context: Tests whether the agent uses @fastify/multipart with explicit limits and throwFileSizeLimit: true, uses import.meta.dirname for static file paths, and writes tests using node:test with Fastify's inject() method.

checklist.name: @fastify/multipart used
checklist.description: Imports and registers `@fastify/multipart` (NOT multer, busboy standalone, or formidable)

checklist.name: Explicit limits object
checklist.description: The @fastify/multipart registration includes a `limits` object with at least two of: fileSize, files, fieldNameSize, parts, fields

checklist.name: import.meta.dirname for static path
checklist.description: Static file serving (or path joining) uses `import.meta.dirname` as the base path (NOT `process.cwd()` or `__dirname`)

checklist.name: node:test imports
checklist.description: Test file imports `describe`, `it`, `before`, and/or `after` from `node:test` (NOT mocha, jest, vitest, or tap)

checklist.name: app.inject() used in tests
checklist.description: Tests call `app.inject()` to simulate HTTP requests (NOT supertest, axios, node-fetch, or actual HTTP)

checklist.name: app.close() in after()
checklist.description: Test file calls `app.close()` inside an `after()` (or afterEach) hook to clean up the server

checklist.name: Logger disabled in tests
checklist.description: Test app is built with `logger: false` (or logger disabled) to keep test output clean

checklist.name: Error case tested
checklist.description: Test suite includes at least one test that checks a non-200 status code (e.g., 400 when no file is provided)
